alt_forms.note: Greek-derived katakana loanword for 'aegis'. Used in Persona 3 (character name) and other JP games drawing on Greek myth. Preferred if Greek mythological framing is intended.

definitions.gloss_en: divine protection or a magical ward; a saving mechanic granting resistance to certain damage types
definitions.gloss_jp: {神|かみ}や{霊|れい}{的|てき}{存|そん}{在|ざい}からの{守|まも}り；{特|とく}{定|てい}のダメージを{軽|けい}{減|げん}する{魔|ま}{法|ほう}{的|てき}な{加|か}{護|ご}
definitions.explanation_en: A protective blessing or ward. In game terms, represents a saving throw or damage reduction granted by divine or magical means.
definitions.implicit_notes: 加護 is a deeply Japanese Shinto and Buddhist concept meaning divine blessing/protection (as in 神の加護, 'divine grace'). Using 加護 for an aegis-type game mechanic grounds the game in Japanese spiritual vocabulary rather than Greek mythology.
definitions.orthography_notes: 加護: 加 (add/grant) + 護 (protect). A classical Japanese Buddhist and Shinto term. Kotobank dictionary cites it as 'divine protection'. Used in Japanese RPGs broadly (JRPGs). Not WH-specific. アイギス is the Greek-mythology katakana alternative.
definitions.game_setting_notes: TRPGs and fantasy board games. 加護 gives the mechanic a Japanese spiritual flavour. アイギス (Greek: aegis, divine shield) is used in games explicitly referencing Greek mythology (Persona 3).

usage_jp: {神|かみ}の{加|か}{護|ご} (divine protection); {加|か}{護|ご}セービング (aegis saving roll)
comparative_notes: 加護 carries Japanese spiritual resonance. アイギス is appropriate for Greek-mythology-framed games. Neither is WH-specific.

examples.japanese: {神|かみ}のご{加|か}{護|ご}がありますように。

cross_references.note: 防御 — defense (general); 加護 is a specific supernatural form of protection
